labs.title: Blue Team Labs: Windows Event Log Analysis
labs.notes: Analyzed Windows Security Event Logs to identify simulated lateral movement. Used Event IDs 4624/4625 (logon success/fail), 4648 (explicit creds), and 4688 (process creation) to reconstruct the attacker timeline. Correlated events across multiple hosts to map the full kill chain.

labs.title: Nmap Network Scanning Fundamentals
labs.notes: Comprehensive Nmap practice: SYN scan (-sS), UDP scan (-sU), version detection (-sV), OS fingerprinting (-O), and the scripting engine (--script=banner,vuln). Practiced timing templates T1-T5 and XML output for report generation. Key insight: SYN scans leave half-open connections — useful for stealth but detectable by modern IDS.

labs.title: OSINT: Trace Labs — Passive Reconnaissance
labs.notes: Passive recon using publicly available sources only. Google dorks (site:, filetype:, inurl:), TheHarvester for email/subdomain harvesting, Shodan for exposed services, and certificate transparency logs (crt.sh). Reinforced why documentation of every OSINT step matters for legal defensibility.

labs.title: Web Security: Burp Suite Fundamentals
labs.notes: Learned Burp Suite's Proxy, Repeater, Intruder, and Decoder tools. Practiced intercepting and modifying HTTP requests to identify SQL injection (UNION-based) and stored XSS vulnerabilities. Deep understanding of HTTP request/response cycle, cookies, and session management is foundational to all web security work.

labs.title: Advent of Cyber 2025 — Days 1–10
labs.notes: Annual beginner-friendly CTF event. Covered OPSEC failures, PCAP traffic analysis (C2 beacon identification), log investigation, OSINT, and basic web exploitation. Favorite challenge: PCAP analysis where identifying C2 beacon timing patterns revealed the attacker's automated callback schedule.
